summary:
The paograph plotting should begin at 6 cm of cervical dilation because this marks an increased risk of progressing into active labor. This level corresponds to clinically recognized thresholds where surveillance for potential delivery becomes more critical during late stages of gestation.

Note: Do not include any commentary beyond what was stated by Agent2; do not add external knowledge or interpretation.
